source_intake_record.raw_question_text: An electric dipole is centered at the origin, with \( \vec{p} \) in the direction of the \( +y \)-axis. Derive an approximate expression for the electric field at a point \( P \) on the \( y \)-axis for which \( y \) is much larger than \( d \). To do this, use the binomial expansion \( (1 + x)^n \approx 1 + nx + n(n - 1)x^2/2 + \dots \) (valid for the case \( |x| < 1 \)).

decision_reasons: question, image, and answer are all present, so core semantics are well supported
asset registry passed with no recorded issues
high image dependency makes it valuable to retain as a visually grounded sample
verifiability is strong because a short-text answer is present and the sample is registered cleanly
no risk flags or obvious recovery concerns are indicated
